data.title: Polymarket Odds Surge for Potential Israeli Strike on Iranian Power Plant
data.description: Polymarket odds for a contract predicting whether Israel will strike an Iranian power plant before April 30 have risen sharply to 96%, marking a 51% increase within 24 hours. According to NS3.AI, this significant rise in odds reflects heightened tensions and speculation surrounding potential military actions. Senior Israeli officials have expressed that Israel considers it premature to initiate a ceasefire, indicating a desire for operations against Iran to persist for at least another month.

data.title: CZ Says Trump Pardon Felt Like 'Vindication,' Praises US Crypto Policy Shift
data.description: Speaking on The Wolf of All Streets Podcast, Binance co-founder Changpeng Zhao (CZ) said he is deeply grateful for President Trump's pardon, describing it as "a level of vindication" and adding that he valued it more than he initially expected. CZ said the shift represents proof that the US can reverse course by 180 degrees, arguing that the previous regulatory direction would have cost the country its crypto and blockchain industry. He identified the internet, blockchain, and AI as the three defining industries of his adult lifetime, warning that any country that misses one of them will be severely disadvantaged. CZ said the US is now leading on crypto regulation but still lacks participation from some of the largest players in the space and adequate liquidity, a gap he said Binance hopes to help close. He expressed conditional optimism about sustained US crypto progress but cautioned that partisan dynamics — where a pro-crypto administration invites opposition attacks on the industry — represent a structural risk.

data.title: Venezuela to Increase Public Sector Salaries Amid Inflation
data.description: Venezuela's interim government plans to raise the monthly salaries of public sector workers to alleviate the pressure of prolonged high inflation and stagnant wage growth on households. According to Jin10, Venezuela's interim President Rodriguez announced that this adjustment will take effect on May 1 and will be implemented 'responsibly.' However, she did not disclose the specific scale of the adjustment. Data from the country's central bank indicates that the Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose by 14.6% in February compared to the previous month, down from 32.6% in January, yet Venezuela remains one of the countries with the fastest inflation rates globally.

data.title: STOCKS | Shenzhen Stock Exchange Adjusts Hong Kong Stock Connect Securities List
data.description: The Shenzhen Stock Exchange has announced an adjustment to the list of securities eligible for trading through the Hong Kong Stock Connect. According to Jin10, the changes will take effect on April 9, 2026, with the inclusion of Xidi Zhijia among the eligible securities. This adjustment reflects ongoing efforts to enhance cross-border investment opportunities and facilitate market access for investors.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 600 USDT with a 2.95% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 09, 2026, 00:30 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 600 USDT and is now trading at 599.669983 USDT, with a narrowed 2.95% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: The Ethereum Foundation has sold 3,750 ETH at an average price of approximately $2,214.
data.description: According to crypto analyst EmberCN, of the 5,000 ETH that the Ethereum Foundation planned to sell in this round, 3,750 have been sold, with a total transaction amount of approximately $8.3 million and an average selling price of approximately $2,214.

data.title: U.S. and Iran Announce Temporary Ceasefire Amid Regional Tensions
data.description: The United States and Iran declared a temporary ceasefire on April 8, with plans to engage in negotiations later this week. According to BlockBeats, despite the ceasefire being announced just a day ago, disagreements have already emerged regarding whether Lebanon is included in the ceasefire zone. Iranian analysts suggest that Israel, viewing Iran as its primary regional adversary, has intensified attacks on Lebanon following the ceasefire, potentially aiming to undermine the agreement and diminish Iran's regional influence. If Israel does not halt its actions against Lebanon soon, there is a possibility that regional hostilities could reignite.

data.title: CZ Says He Chose to Face US Charges to Protect Binance, BNB Holders, and the Crypto Industry
data.description: Speaking on The Wolf of All Streets Podcast, Binance founder Changpeng Zhao (CZ) said he chose to travel to the United States to face legal proceedings — rather than remain in the UAE, where he said he could have stayed indefinitely — primarily to shield Binance, its users, and the broader crypto industry from the risk of a corporate indictment and its consequences for BNB holders. CZ said he did not expect to receive a prison sentence, as his lawyers found no precedent for a single Bank Secrecy Act violation resulting in imprisonment. He had anticipated a period of home confinement, comparable to the outcome for BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes. The Department of Justice, under the Biden administration, requested a 36-month sentence just one week before his April 30, 2024 sentencing hearing — more than double what CZ said the most severe application of sentencing guidelines would have produced, a point the judge acknowledged. The judge ultimately sentenced CZ to four months. CZ described the mental stress of not knowing what would happen next as the most difficult aspect of the entire period.

data.title: CZ: I Was 'Just a Coder' at Bloomberg and Tokyo Exchange, Had No Idea What Binance Would Become
data.description: Speaking on The Wolf of All Streets Podcast, Binance co-founder Changpeng Zhao said he had no expectation during his early career at Bloomberg and the Tokyo Stock Exchange that he would build a company of Binance's scale. He described those years as focused on developing technical and professional foundations — coding, being a team contributor, learning workplace discipline. Zhao drew a clear distinction between those formative years and the leadership and company-building capabilities that he said came only later in his career.

data.title: Canary Capital Files for Canary PEPE ETF Registration
data.description: Canary Capital has submitted an S-1 registration statement for the Canary PEPE ETF, according to ChainCatcher. The filing with the SEC indicates plans to launch the ETF once the registration becomes effective. Previously, the firm faced skepticism for applying for numerous altcoin ETFs, with some questioning whether the moves were merely promotional.

data.title: Polymarket's probability that "Israel will take military action against Iran's nuclear power plant before April 30" has risen to 96%, a 51% increase in the past 24 hours.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the probability of Israel taking military action against Iranian nuclear power plants before April 30th has risen to 96% on Polymarket, a 51% increase in 24 hours. The contract rules state that if Israel launches a military strike against any Iranian power plant before April 30th, the market will mark it as "yes." Otherwise, it will mark it as "no." This includes, but is not limited to, drone and missile attacks, aerial bombing, and military operations by Israeli ground personnel. Cyberattacks, sanctions, or diplomatic actions are not considered in resolving disputes in this market. Israeli airstrikes, missile attacks, or drone attacks that are intercepted, shot down, or miss their targets will not be considered in this market. Senior Israeli officials have stated that Israel believes a ceasefire is premature and hopes the military action against Iran can continue for at least another month. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing them in.

data.title: New Zealand Central Bank Chair Comments on Economic Stimulus
data.description: New Zealand Reserve Bank Chair Breman stated that previous interest rate cuts continue to provide some stimulus to the economy. According to Jin10, Breman emphasized the ongoing impact of these monetary policy measures, suggesting that they are still contributing to economic activity. The remarks highlight the central bank's approach to managing economic growth and stability through interest rate adjustments.

data.title: The U.S. Treasury Department plans to introduce new anti-money laundering rules for stablecoins and strengthen sanctions compliance requirements.
data.description: The U.S. Treasury Department's FinCEN and OFAC are planning to jointly release rules requiring stablecoin issuers to establish robust anti-money laundering and sanctions compliance systems. These systems will include freezing, blocking, and rejecting suspicious transactions, and complying with the Bank Secrecy Act. The rules emphasize a risk-and-effect approach, arguing that institutions with sound compliance systems typically reduce enforcement risks. This move aims to advance the implementation of the GENIUS Act, which is expected to fully take effect in 2027. (CoinDesk)

data.title: CZ Reflects on Pre-Bitcoin Career in Financial Tech: 'I Just Didn't Think About It'
data.description: Speaking on The Wolf of All Streets Podcast about his new memoir, Binance founder Changpeng Zhao said he did not understand money until he encountered Bitcoin, despite having spent years working in financial technology, including on trading systems at Bloomberg and the Tokyo Stock Exchange. Zhao said his conviction in Bitcoin and blockchain stems not from a finance background but from a technology framework: he measures innovations by whether they improve efficiency without meaningful trade-offs, citing incremental improvements in living standards — from bare cement floors to running water to heated taps — as examples of the same principle. "I think blockchain is a huge technology," he said.

data.title: Iranian media: The Strait of Hormuz is now completely closed.
data.description: According to Iran's Press TV, the Strait of Hormuz has been completely closed, forcing oil tankers to return to port. (Jinshi)

data.title: NBA Regular Season: Hawks vs. Cavaliers Prediction Market Activity
data.description:
A recent prediction market event on Polymarket has seen significant activity regarding the NBA regular season game between the Atlanta Hawks and the Cleveland Cavaliers. According to Odaily, an account with a win rate exceeding 55% has invested $120,000 on the Hawks defeating the Cavaliers, with an opening price of 39¢.

The game commenced at 7 a.m. UTC+8 and is approaching halftime. The NBA regular season is nearing its conclusion, with the Hawks holding a record of 45 wins and 34 losses, placing them fifth in the Eastern Conference. Meanwhile, the Cavaliers have a record of 50 wins and 29 losses, ranking fourth in the Eastern Conference.

data.title: Federal Reserve Officials Consider Rate Adjustments Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Federal Reserve officials are evaluating various scenarios for the U.S. economy following the outbreak of war in Iran. According to PANews, the minutes from the March FOMC meeting, released on Wednesday, reveal that most officials are concerned about the potential impact of the conflict on the labor market, which may necessitate a reduction in interest rates. However, many officials also highlighted inflation risks, which could ultimately require rate hikes. The minutes indicate that an increasing number of officials recommend including statements in post-meeting communications about the possibility of rate increases under specific conditions. The minutes state, 'Some participants believe there is sufficient reason to describe future rate decisions in a two-way manner in the post-meeting statement, reflecting that raising the target range for rates may be appropriate if inflation remains above target levels.'

data.title: Non-Dollar Stablecoins and the Illusion of Currency Change
data.description: Non-dollar stablecoins often create the illusion that changing the unit of account equates to altering the currency system. According to PANews, the true value lies not in the label but in the infrastructure and control mechanisms behind it.

data.title: Foreign Investment in Japanese Stocks Surges
data.description: Foreign investors purchased Japanese stocks worth 29,596 billion yen in the week leading up to April 3, according to Jin10. This marks a significant turnaround from the previous week's figure of -44,481 billion yen, indicating renewed interest in Japan's equity market.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI's Enterprise Revenue Surges, Expected to Match Consumer Business by 2026
data.description: OpenAI's Chief Revenue Officer, Denise Dresser, announced that the company's enterprise business now accounts for over 40% of its total revenue. According to Jin10, this growth is built on the strong performance of its consumer business. Dresser projected that by the end of 2026, the enterprise segment is expected to equal the consumer business in revenue contribution. Additionally, OpenAI's Codex has recently surpassed 3 million weekly active users.
